learning_outcomes.description: Employ interaction design methods to inform the development of interactive technologies
Develop solutions to design problems through the creative application of interaction design methods and processes.
Create designs in the context of a large open-ended project, taking into account other, potentially conflicting, features.
Analyse complex problems and apply design patterns to design contextually appropriate solutions.
Demonstrate an ability to work collaboratively and effectively with others in a team towards shared project goals, while managing individual work

assessments.task_description:
As a part of this assessment, students are asked to develop a proposal of their project, especially focussing on 1) interaction mechanisms, and 2) ethical implications. You will write a ~1500 word essay on their project proposal.

AI Use

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 1 grade for each 24 hour period from time submission is due will apply for up to 7 days. After 7 days you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
Students forming teams and working on their projects is at the core of Studio-2 Interaction Design. As a part of this assessment, student teams will identify a problem space and develop a prototype solution, using various Interaction Design methods and techniques. Teams will follow a complete design cycle of exploring and understanding a problem, eliciting user requirements, creating engaging interaction designs, developing prototypes and testing/evaluating them in real-world situations. 

Teams will conduct two iterations of their prototypes and showcase their design prototypes and processes in week 8 (first iteration) and in week 13 (second iteration). Teams will submit a detailed project report representing their design and development processes.

This team-based assessment will be divided into three smaller-assessment milestones:

  1. Design Exhibit/Presentation of Iteration 1 (week 8): 10%
  2. Design Exhibit of Iteration 2 (week 13): 25%
  3. Project Report (13 Nov 2026): 15%

Milestone 1 is expected to be a work-in-progress activity, where a prototype will be exhibited during a regular studio session. It is expected that prototypes developed in this milestone are interactive and present the project ideas clearly. Prototypes may not be fully developed at this stage and there is no expectation of any user evaluations/testing in this milestone.

Milestone 2 is expected to show a final prototype, where the aesthetics and look-and-feel of prototypes is high-resolution. It is expected that findings from an adequate level of user engagement, testing or evaluation is incorporated in the prototype. 

Milestone 3 is expected to be a ~3000-word project report that should capture all aspects of the project.

Use of AI - Milestone 1 and 2

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

Use of AI - Milestone 3

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Milestone 3 submission via the course Blackboard site

assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.due_date:
9/11/2026 - 13/11/2026

In-person interviews will take place in Exam Week 1. Sign-up for sessions will be available from Week 12 to assist your planning around exams. As this is a secure, identity-verified assessment, you will need to produce valid UQ identification before commencing your interview.
assessments.task_description:
As a precursor to this assessment, students are asked to maintain a design journal throughout this studio course and update it on a weekly basis. Students should report their project and design activities in their journals and provide evidence. A minimum of 9 journal entries are required, where students need to provide the following content on each journal entry: 1) a description of the design activity, 2) visual evidence of the activity through images, and 3) a statement on learning from the design activity. 

During the in-person interviews, you will have 5-7 minutes to present the highlights from your journal, followed by a 15-20 minutes of Q&A session with teaching staff. The aim of this assessment is to gain an in-depth understanding of the contributions your made to the team project and your learnings from this activity. In the Q&A session, the questions will be centred around the activities you reported in your design journal entries. If a student is unable to provide clear evidence of their individual contribution to their team project or is unable to answer questions related to the design journal entries, their final grades will be capped at 3. As this is a hurdle assessment, if a student receives a grade of 3 in this assessment, they will FAIL this course.

AI Use:

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

In accordance with UQ Assessment Policy, your presentation will be recorded.

assessments.task_description:
This assessment involves writing a review on a research paper that focuses on Interaction Design topics. A list of papers will be given out to students, via the Blackboard site. It is expected that the review is around ~1500 words and discusses the positive and negative aspects of the paper. 

AI Use

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct
